utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , bryan . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well . i'm a little sore .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , well , i know the nurse told you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so bryan is a 55-year-old male with a past medical history significant for prior discectomy , who presents with back pain . so , bryan , what happened to your back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you ... my wife made me push a , uh , refrigerator out through the other room , and when i was helping to move it , i felt something in my back on the lower right side .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , on the lower right side of this back ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . those wives , always making you do stuff .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and what day did this happen on ? how long ago ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , this was about five days ago .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: five days ago .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and , you know , i have that history of discectomy .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i'm just worried that something happened .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and , and what have you taken for the pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , i have , uh , been taking ibuprofen . uh , and i tried once tylenol and ibuprofen at the same time , and that gave me some relief .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and have you had any symptoms like pain in your legs or numbing or tingling ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , no , nothing significant like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , just the pain in your back .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just the pain in the back . it hurts to bend over .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and any problems with your bladder or your bowels ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i know the nurse said to review a symptom sheet when you checked in .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and i know that you were endorsing the back pain . any other symptoms ? chest pain ? shortness of breath ? abdominal pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: nausea ? vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no other symptoms .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam . hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so your vital signs here in the office look really good . you do n't have a fever . your blood pressure's nice and controlled . so that ... that's good . i'm just gon na check out your back and your heart and your lungs , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so on physical examination , you know , your heart sounds great . there's ... it's a regular rate and rhythm . your lungs are nice and clear . on your back exam , you do have some pain to palpation of the right lumbar spine , uh , in the paraspinal muscles along with decreased flexion and extension of the back , and you have a positive straight leg on the right . or positive straight leg raise on the right , uh , but your strength is good bilaterally in your lower extremities . so that means that i think that you've injured your back .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , but , you know , i think it's something that we can , we can fix , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , you do n't think there's anything wrong with the ... where i had the surgery before .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i do n't think so .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: let's took at some of your results . hey , dragon , show me the back x-ray . so this is an x-ray of your lumbar spine . you know , there's good bony , bony alignment . i do n't see any fracture or anything like that . so that's a good sign . um , hey , dragon . show me the labs . and your labs here all look good , so i'm , i'm happy to see that . uh , so let's talk a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i ... my assessment for your first problem , your back pain . i think you have a lumbar strain . i do n't think that anything else is going on , but i wan na go ahead and order an mri-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just to be sure .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ? and then i'm gon na prescribe you some meloxicam 15 milligrams once a day along with some ultram , 50 milligrams every four hours as needed , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , and then we'll go ahead and refer you to some physical therapy once we get the mri results back , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: should i continue to take the tylenol and the ibuprofen ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you can stop the ibuprofen .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you can take tylenol if you want .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you know to call me if , if you need anything .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any questions , uh , bryan ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no questions .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: Bryan Smith is a 55-year-old male with a history of prior discectomy presenting with back pain.
Back pain
The doctor asks the patient to describe the onset of his back pain.
The patient reports lower right-sided back pain after pushing a refrigerator five days ago.
The doctor asks how long ago the back injury occurred.
The injury occurred five days ago.
The patient has a history of a discectomy.
The patient is concerned that the injury affected his previous surgical site.
The doctor asks what medications the patient has taken for the pain.
The patient has been taking ibuprofen and found relief when combining it with Tylenol.
The doctor asks if the patient has leg pain, numbness, or tingling.
The patient denies leg pain, numbness, or tingling.
The patient reports pain when bending over.
The doctor asks if the patient has any bladder or bowel problems.
The patient denies bladder or bowel problems.
The doctor asks about other symptoms including chest pain, shortness of breath, and abdominal pain.
The patient denies chest pain, shortness of breath, and abdominal pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has nausea or vomiting.
The patient denies nausea and vomiting.
The doctor notes the patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam shows normal heart and lung sounds, right lumbar spine tenderness, decreased back flexion and extension, positive straight leg raise on the right, and good lower extremity strength.
The patient asks if the injury is related to his previous discectomy.
The doctor states he does not believe the injury is related to the previous discectomy.
Lumbar spine X-ray shows normal alignment with no fractures, and labs are within normal limits.
The doctor assesses the patient with a lumbar strain and plans to order an MRI.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam 15 mg daily and Ultram 50 mg every 4 hours as needed.
The doctor will refer the patient to physical therapy after MRI results are received.
The patient asks if he should continue taking Tylenol and ibuprofen.
The doctor advises the patient to discontinue ibuprofen.
The doctor advises the patient that he may continue taking Tylenol.
